title: Error SP030 on "filter((?x<1)&&(?x>0))" on ">"
description:
This filter query on http://live.dbpedia.org/sparql results in an error:
select ?x
{
 ?s ?p ?x.
 filter((?x<1)&&(?x>0))
} limit 1

Replacing "(?x>0)" with "(?x<0)" or "(?x > 0)" works though.
The full error:
Virtuoso 37000 Error SP030: SPARQL compiler, line 6: syntax error at '<1)&&(?x>' before '0'

SPARQL query:
define sql:big-data-const 0
#output-format:text/html
define sql:signal-void-variables 1 define input:default-graph-uri <http://dbpedia.org> select ?x
{
?s ?p ?x.
filter((?x<1)&&(?x>0))
} limit 1

Virtuoso version 07.20.3214 on Linux (i686-generic-linux-glibc212-64), Single Server Edition
key_bug_features: filter with conflicting inequalities on variable ?x
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value FLOAT NOT NULL);
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE value < 1 AND value > 0;
CREATE TABLE another_table (ref_id INT, description VARCHAR(255), FOREIGN KEY (ref_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
INSERT INTO another_table (ref_id, description) VALUES (1, 'Valid Entry'), (2, 'Another Valid Entry');
